- The Tombstone wiring + content hash + audit event emission (memory.* events) that landed alongside the interface are real value. Reverting drops them.
- The export/import functionality (memory.Export / memory.Import) is built on top.

Against:
- Dead abstraction in the sense that no second implementation is planned. mnemos.Client was meant to be that second implementation; Mnemos-as-plugin removes the motivation.
- Two memory-adjacent paths (Manager for REST CRUD + EmbeddedClient for runtime) creates a divergence risk. REST CRUD writes still go through Manager → nomi.db; runtime writes go through EmbeddedClient → same store but via the abstraction. Reads diverge if anyone forgets which side they're on.

Scope of this feature:
- Audit every call site (planner.go, lifecycle.go, engine.go, internal/api/memory.go) and document who depends on what.
- Decide: keep, simplify, or revert.
  Option A: Keep as-is. Document the abstraction's purpose in internal/memstore/doc.go more clearly ("typed memory boundary; not a future external interface").
  Option B: Merge memstore + EmbeddedClient back into *memory.Manager. Manager grows the typed Scope API; runtime + REST CRUD share one type. Lose ~400 LOC; gain simpler import graph.
  Option C: Keep memstore.Client as the only memory interface, port REST CRUD to it, delete *memory.Manager. Most invasive; eliminates divergence entirely.
- Whichever option lands, preserve the audit events (memory.store / memory.forget / memory.tombstone) and the export/import wire format.

Acceptance:
- Decision recorded as a short note in docs/adr/ or as a comment block in internal/memory/doc.go.
- 41 packages still green under go test -race ./...
- No regression in audit-chain coverage for memory operations.

Depends on: nothing.
Relates to: ADR 0004 revised, commits ac42baf, 0d9b414, 4bf31da.

tasks.title: Implement Wire plugins.Registry.ContextSources() into the planner prompt
tasks.description:
Implement the feature: Wire plugins.Registry.ContextSources() into the planner prompt. Consume context sources at plan time. The plugins registry already collects ContextSource instances across registered plugins (internal/plugins/registry.go), and the ContextSource.Query interface now carries RunID (commit bde5ccf), but nothing in internal/runtime/planner.go actually invokes them at run start. Result: the Mnemos plugin's claimsContextSource exists, is configurable, has a working stub-server unit test, and produces zero output when an assistant uses it. Same for Obsidian's vaultContextSource and any future context source.

Scope:
- Add a step in the planner's prompt-assembly phase (likely in internal/runtime/planner.go near where folder context is currently spliced in) that:
  1. Looks up the assistant's bound connections (existing assistant_connection_bindings table).
  2. For each binding with role="context_source", finds the matching ContextSource on the plugin registry.
  3. Calls Query(ctx, plugins.ContextQueryRequest{RunID, Goal, MaxTokens}).
  4. Wraps the result in the existing wrapUntrusted("plugin_context", ...) trust-boundary tag and appends to the context block the planner already builds.
- Errors from Query are non-fatal — log + continue, don't fail the run (existing pattern for folder context).
- Respect a sane MaxTokens cap so a chatty context source can't blow the planner context budget. Pull from MaxContextBytes / similar that the planner already enforces.
- Add a test in internal/runtime that:
  - Wires a fake plugin with a ContextSource implementation that returns a known string.
  - Creates a run with an assistant bound to that plugin's connection in role=context_source.
  - Asserts the planner prompt (or step input, whichever it lands on) contains the known string wrapped in plugin_context tags.

Out of scope:
- Per-source priority / ordering. First-implementation can be insertion order; an explicit priority field on the binding is a follow-up.
- Visibility caps per assistant — a Mnemos-specific concern that can layer on once the plumbing exists.
- Streaming partial context — Query returns the full string today; chunked context delivery is a future optimization.

Acceptance:
- Test asserts that a run with a Mnemos-bound assistant + a stub Mnemos server returns a planner prompt containing the rendered Context Block.
- Folder context still works (no behavior regression).
- A run for an assistant with no context-source bindings produces the same prompt it does today (no spurious empty sections).

Depends on: #115 (ContextSource.Query takes ContextQueryRequest) — landed in bde5ccf. This is the consumer side of that interface change; without it, #115's plumbing is dormant.

Relates to: roady #113 (Mnemos plugin), commits bde5ccf, dd58ae6.

tasks.title: Implement Add enum/options support to plugins.ConfigField for dropdown UI
tasks.description:
Implement the feature: Add enum/options support to plugins.ConfigField for dropdown UI. Extend the plugin manifest's ConfigField type with an optional enum/options list so the desktop UI can render a <select> dropdown for fixed-choice fields. Today every config field renders as a free-text Input, which is fine for base_url but suboptimal for visibility_default (personal | team | org), poll-interval presets, log-level selectors, and similar.

Concrete motivation: the Mnemos plugin's visibility_default field (commit dd58ae6) is rendered as a free-text input. A user typo silently produces an invalid value the server later rejects. A dropdown UX gives both validation and discovery.

Scope:
- internal/plugins/manifest.go — extend ConfigField:
    type ConfigField struct {
      Type        string   `json:"type"`     // "string" | "boolean" | "number" | "enum"
      Label       string   `json:"label"`
      Required    bool     `json:"required"`
      Default     string   `json:"default,omitempty"`
      Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
      Options     []ConfigOption `json:"options,omitempty"` // populated when Type == "enum"
    }
    type ConfigOption struct {
      Value string `json:"value"`
      Label string `json:"label,omitempty"` // human-readable; falls back to Value
    }
- internal/plugins/mnemos/manifest.go — update visibility_default to use Type: "enum" with three options (personal, team, org). Keep Default: "team".
- Update any other plugin manifest that could benefit (audit telegram, gmail, etc. — most plugins won't need it, leave them alone).
- app/src/components/plugins-manager.tsx — in the AddConnectionDialog form rendering, branch on field.type === "enum" → render a <select> populated from field.options instead of an <Input>. Selected value defaults to field.default if present.
- Playwright test in app/e2e/mnemos-plugin.spec.ts asserts that visibility_default surfaces with type "enum" and the three options.
- Unit test in internal/plugins for the manifest schema serialization.

Out of scope:
- Multi-select inputs. Single-select only; multi can layer later if a plugin needs it.
- Conditional fields ("show X only when Y is selected"). Static schema for v1.
- Numeric enums (Type: "enum" with numeric values). String-only for the first pass.

Acceptance:
- New AddConnectionDialog renders a dropdown for Mnemos visibility_default.
- The dropdown defaults to "team" matching the manifest's Default.
- Existing string/number/boolean fields render unchanged.
- A plugin with a malformed enum (Type: "enum" + empty Options) loads as before but the UI surfaces a clear validation warning instead of rendering an empty dropdown.

Depends on: nothing.
Relates to: roady #113, #116. Cross-cutting change touching the plugin manifest contract.

tasks.title: Implement Verify e2e-mnemos workflow once via workflow_dispatch
tasks.description:
Implement the feature: Verify e2e-mnemos workflow once via workflow_dispatch. Trigger the .github/workflows/e2e-mnemos.yml workflow once manually to confirm CI plumbing works end to end against `mnemos serve` at the pinned v0.15.3 ref. Catches issues that only surface in the GitHub Actions environment: cache hits, working-directory drift, mnemos serve startup quirks on ubuntu-latest, port-collision behavior under the GitHub runner network, etc.

Why this isn't automatic:
- The workflow was added in commit 62a99e1. Triggers fire on pushes/PRs touching internal/plugins/mnemos/** or cmd/nomid/main.go, plus manual workflow_dispatch.
- A green run on a real PR is the canonical proof. The first PR that touches plugin code will exercise it; this feature is the deliberate verification before that organic test happens, so we know the workflow itself isn't broken when we actually need it.

Scope:
- gh workflow run e2e-mnemos --ref main (or via the GitHub UI).
- Inspect the run: confirm mnemos serve started, /health came up within 30s, all 6 tool calls + the context source roundtripped, server log got dumped to the workflow output.
- If anything fails, fix the workflow in-place rather than re-pinning Mnemos to a different version (the pin is correct; CI quirks are the suspect).
- Write a short note in docs/runbooks/ (or extend docs/vendor-notes.md) summarizing what to look for next time the workflow trips so the failure mode is documented.
- Pin the green-run URL in this feature's notes for future reference.

Acceptance:
- The workflow shows a green run in the GitHub Actions tab.
- The mnemos.log artifact (or stdout tail) is captured and inspectable.
- TestE2E_FullRoundTrip's 7 sub-steps all pass against the live server.
- Total runtime <90s (the budget the workflow's timeout-minutes: 10 assumes plenty of headroom for).

Depends on: nothing. The workflow + test are already in main as of commit 62a99e1.

Relates to: roady #117. Not blocking any other feature; pure CI hygiene.

tasks.title: Implement Sandboxed executor backends
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Sandboxed executor backends. Pluggable execution backend for `command.exec` and tool side-effects. Current implementation runs on the host with approval gating only — competitors (NanoClaw, Hermes) provide container isolation. Introduce `Executor` interface with backends: `local` (current behavior, default), `docker` (rootless container per-run with bind-mounted workspace, network policy, CPU/mem limits), and `gvisor` (kernel-isolated runtime for stronger boundary). Each Run pins a backend via assistant config or run override. Backend choice surfaces in event stream and audit log so approval reviewers see isolation level. Filesystem writes constrained to the bind-mount; outbound network gated by `network.egress` capability rules in `PermissionPolicy`. Includes Tauri settings UI for backend selection, golden tests verifying container cleanup, and Prometheus metrics per backend (exec duration, exit codes, OOM kills). Closes the biggest security gap vs NanoClaw/Hermes while preserving the local-first deployment story.

tasks.title: Implement Connector parity wave — Discord, Slack, WhatsApp
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Connector parity wave — Discord, Slack, WhatsApp. Ship three additional messaging connectors via the existing `connectors.Connector` plugin SDK to close the integration gap vs Hermes (6+ messengers), OpenClaw, and NanoClaw. Implement `discord` (bot user + slash commands + threads → Run), `slack` (Events API + Socket Mode + per-channel assistant binding), and `whatsapp` (Cloud API w/ webhook receiver + media handling). Each connector: persists config via `ConnectorConfigRepository` (no env vars), implements message→Run mapping with assistant routing rules, surfaces in Settings → Connections tab w/ OAuth/token flow, emits domain events through `eventBus.Publish`, supports outbound replies w/ rate limiting + retry/backoff, and ships e2e tests against a recorded fixture. Includes a shared `messaging` helper package for common concerns (attachment download, signed-webhook verification, idempotent dedupe of inbound messages by provider message ID).

tasks.title: Implement Scheduled runs with natural-language cron
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Scheduled runs with natural-language cron. First-class `Schedule` aggregate that triggers Runs on cron or natural-language cadences ("every weekday at 8am", "first Monday of the month"). Matches Hermes Agent's flagship feature for always-on workflows. Adds `schedules` table (id, assistant_id, prompt, cron_expr, nl_phrase, next_fire_at, last_fire_at, enabled, created_at), repository, REST endpoints (`POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE /schedules`), and a background ticker in `nomid` that scans `next_fire_at <= now` every 30s and triggers Runs through the runtime with `triggered_by: schedule`. NL→cron translation via a small LLM call w/ allowlisted output grammar + human confirmation in the Tauri settings UI before save. Schedules respect the assistant's `PermissionPolicy` — schedules cannot bypass approval gates. Includes pause/resume, missed-fire policy (skip vs catch-up), Prometheus counters per schedule, audit log entries, and timezone handling tied to the host TZ.

tasks.title: Implement Recipe registry and sharing
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Recipe registry and sharing. Promote `examples/coding-agent` to a first-class artifact: a `Recipe` is a versioned bundle of (assistant config, permission policy, tool registrations, planner prompts, optional folder context shape). Add `recipes` table + REST endpoints (`GET /recipes`, `POST /recipes/install`, `POST /recipes/export`), a Tauri "Recipes" tab to browse/install/export, signed manifest format (`recipe.yaml` + SHA-256), and a small built-in catalog (coding-agent, research-assistant, ops-runbook). Install flow creates assistant + applies permission policy + registers any recipe-shipped tools, with a preview-diff step so the user sees exactly what the recipe grants before confirming. Export bundles the current assistant's config for shareable distribution. Establishes the surface that future skill-induction (separate feature) will publish into.

tasks.title: Implement Skill induction from run history
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Skill induction from run history. Hermes-style auto-generated skills: mine successful Runs in Mnemos memory + state store, cluster by goal embedding similarity + tool-call shape, and synthesize reusable Recipe candidates with parameterized prompts and named slots for variable inputs. Surfaces "Suggested skills" panel in the Assistants tab — user reviews extracted skill, can edit prompts/policies, then promotes to a Recipe via the registry. Includes guardrails: minimum N successful runs per cluster before suggestion, no PII/secret leakage (scan parameterized slots against secret patterns), and provenance trail linking the skill back to the source Runs. Optional periodic background job (gated by a setting) re-mines weekly. Depends on the recipe registry feature.

tasks.title: Implement Natural-language cron translation
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Natural-language cron translation. LLM-driven translator from natural-language schedule phrases ("every weekday at 8am", "first Monday of the month") to standard 5-field cron expressions, gated by the scheduler's cron parser before persistence. Closes the gap vs Hermes Agent's flagship scheduling UX. Adds `POST /schedules/translate` returning the parsed cron + a human-readable explanation, plus an `nl_phrase` column on the schedules table so the UI can re-display the original phrase next to the persisted cron. Validation is two-stage: the LLM is asked for a strict JSON object via the existing structured-output prompt pattern, then the returned cron_expr is verified with the same robfig/cron parser the scheduler uses at fire time. Confidence + explanation surface in the UI so the user can confirm before save.

tasks.title: Implement Desktop UI surfaces for schedules, skills, recipe export
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Desktop UI surfaces for schedules, skills, recipe export. Ships the deferred Tauri UI panels for three backend features that landed without front-end surfaces: (1) Schedules tab — NL phrase input wired to /schedules/translate with parsed-cron confirmation step before save, schedule list with enable toggle + delete; (2) Skill suggestions panel inside the Recipes tab — fetches /skills/suggestions on demand and surfaces clusters with a one-click promote button that hits /skills/promote; (3) Recipe export button on the assistant editor that calls /recipes/export and opens a dialog with the resulting YAML for copy/download. Each surface is built as a focused component that consumes the existing TypeScript API client; no new backend work.

tasks.title: Implement LLM-driven skill synthesis
tasks.description: Implement the feature: LLM-driven skill synthesis. Augment heuristic skill induction with an LLM-driven synthesis step: given a cluster of similar past runs, ask the configured LLM provider to propose a Recipe shape (suggested name, system_prompt extracted as a reusable template, capability set inferred from the runs' step plans). Output goes through the same JSON-envelope + validation pipeline used by NL cron translation. Surfaces as POST /skills/synthesize/:suggestion_id and a "Generate recipe" button in the skill suggestions panel that pre-fills the promote form. Keeps Jaccard clustering as the cheap default; richer embedding-based clustering is a follow-up that plugs into the same Synthesize path.

tasks.title: Implement Embeddings + auto-learning loop
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Embeddings + auto-learning loop. Three coupled changes that close the "self-learning" gap vs Hermes while preserving the reviewable-agents wedge: (1) Embedding client interface + OpenAI-compat implementation so any embeddings-capable provider can serve Nomi's semantic-recall paths (Ollama, OpenAI, Together, vLLM). Anthropic skipped — they have no native embeddings. (2) Skill induction uses cosine similarity over goal embeddings when an embedding client is configured; falls back to Jaccard otherwise. Threshold tunable; both code paths share the cluster→synthesis→promote pipeline. (3) Auto-extracted user preferences: on RunCompleted, a background extractor asks the LLM to surface short reusable preference statements ("user prefers running tests before commits", "always use yarn over npm") and writes them to memstore.LocalPreferences. Planner already consumes that surface; this closes the loop so future runs reflect learned preferences without user editing. UI surface in the Memory tab shows inferred preferences with a delete button — keeps the wedge: agent learns, user stays in control.

tasks.title: Implement Recipe catalog expansion — 6 new built-in recipes
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Recipe catalog expansion — 6 new built-in recipes. Ships six new built-in Recipes covering prosumer + small-team workflows the wedge actually serves: inbox-triage (read-only email summarizer), release-notes-drafter (read git log + draft changelog from a workspace), meeting-summarizer (read transcript files + extract action items), content-creator (draft blog posts from notes folder), codebase-explorer (read-only repo walk), daily-standup (summarize yesterday's commits + tasks). Each is a curated YAML manifest under internal/recipes/builtin/ with capabilities scoped to the minimum needed (most read-only, one or two scoped writes). Existing Recipe registry + install/export flow consumes them without code changes. Grows the install-flywheel surface from 3 to 9 entries.

tasks.title: Implement FTS5 memory search
tasks.description: Implement the feature: FTS5 memory search. Replace the current case-insensitive LIKE substring scan in `memory.Manager.Search` / `*db.MemoryRepository.Search` with SQLite FTS5. Adds a `memory_fts` virtual table (contentless, indexed on `content`) with INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers that keep it in sync with `memory`. Search uses `MATCH` and falls back to LIKE when the FTS index is unavailable (in-memory DB without FTS5 module, broken trigger state on legacy rows). Honor `Query.Limit`. Add an indexer migration that backfills the FTS table from existing rows on first boot post-upgrade. Cosmetic UX: ranking by `bm25(memory_fts)` so the most relevant entries surface first instead of newest-first when a query is present.

tasks.title: Implement Push notifications for pending approvals
tasks.description: Implement the feature: Push notifications for pending approvals. Surface approval requests through OS notifications so the user doesn't need the Nomi window focused to see when an agent is asking. Adds tauri-plugin-notification (built-in for Tauri 2.x) wired to a new useApprovalNotifications hook that subscribes to approval.requested events and fires a notification with the capability + assistant name + a click-to-focus action. Falls back to the Web Notification API outside Tauri (vite preview / Playwright). Includes a per-app permission prompt on first run + a Settings toggle to disable notifications. Closes the loop: "agents that ask before they act" works much better when the user knows when the agent is asking.

tasks.title: Assistant Builder — Capability Ceiling and Policy Coherence
tasks.description: Prevent silent-deny trap: when a user adds a policy rule (allow/confirm) for a capability whose family is not in the assistant's declared capabilities ceiling, the runtime silently denies. Implement builder validation + API guard to make this failure loud at write time.

tasks.title: Add folder context preview endpoint
tasks.description: POST /tools/filesystem.context/preview endpoint that takes a path and returns the folder tree without requiring a run. Used by the UI for preview before saving assistant.

tasks.title: Discord ChannelProvider + slash commands
tasks.description: DM + guild channel mentions → Conversation + Run. Replies in-thread. Slash-command surface for common operations (/nomi-new, /nomi-approve). Identity allowlist integration.

tasks.title: SQLite schema design and golang-migrate setup
tasks.description: Create migration files for all tables: runs, steps, assistants, events, memory, permissions. Set up golang-migrate in the project. Create migration runner in Go. Include indexes and foreign keys. Create db package with connection management.

tasks.title: Browser session persistence + approval gating
tasks.description: Browser sessions scoped to (assistant_id, session_id); cleaned up after idle timeout. browser.navigate + browser.interact default to confirm mode with per-domain override (e.g. allow google.com navigate but confirm bank.example.com). URL-to-capability mapping for fine-grained gating.

tasks.title: Obsidian ContextSourceProvider: vault indexing
tasks.description: At plan time, expose relevant notes based on the assistant's current goal. Follows [[wikilinks]], filters by frontmatter tags, respects .obsidianignore. Similar shape to today's folder context but vault-aware.

tasks.title: OutboundMessage + Attachment types; Channel.Send signature change
tasks.description: Introduce plugins.OutboundMessage carrying Text + []Attachment. Change Channel.Send signature to accept OutboundMessage. Update every existing channel plugin (Telegram/Email/Slack/Discord) at the call-site level to accept the new signature — implementations keep the current behavior (text-only) until their respective tasks below land the attachment handling. Add a small SendText(ctx, id, text) convenience so runtime callers that don't yet build attachments have a one-line call path.

tasks.title: Observability Baseline and Incident Signals
tasks.description: Implement structured logging baselines, key SLIs, alert thresholds, and incident-grade dashboards/runbooks for daemon, plugin lifecycle jobs, and event streaming.

tasks.title: Per-connection policy + rate limits + approval copy
tasks.description: Extend PermissionPolicy with per_connection_overrides map. Extend ratelimit.go with per-connection budgets (in addition to per-run). Approval payload and plain-language copy include connection display name. Remove the Gmail-as-connector stub from internal/connectors/google/ (oauth.go migrates to internal/integrations/google/ in Gmail plugin task). connection_not_bound error at tool-call time when assistant lacks a binding.

tasks.title: Discord bot + Gateway WebSocket
tasks.description: Discord bot token auth. Gateway WebSocket for real-time events (messages, slash commands, button interactions). Per-bot connection model.

tasks.title: Wire folder context into runtime execution
tasks.description: In runtime.CreateRun or executeStep, read assistant.Contexts, invoke FolderContextTool for any 'folder' attachments, and inject the tree into the run's initial step context or system prompt.

tasks.title: Wazero integration + smoke-test WASM plugin
tasks.description: Add github.com/tetratelabs/wazero. New internal/plugins/wasmhost/ package wraps a wazero runtime + module loader. Define the WASM ABI (plugin_manifest, plugin_configure, plugin_start, plugin_stop + per-role tools/channels/triggers/context_sources + dispatch tool_execute/channel_send/trigger_start). Build a tiny example WASM plugin (TinyGo or AssemblyScript) implementing a single 'echo' tool — round-trip a string through the host. Lives at examples/wasm-plugin-echo/. Validates the ABI shape end-to-end before lifecycle-05 freezes it.

tasks.title: Wire connectors into application lifecycle
tasks.description: Wire ConnectorRegistry into main.go lifecycle: instantiate registry, register Telegram connector, StartAll() after API server, StopAll() on shutdown signal.

tasks.title: Calendar TriggerProvider: pre-meeting + event lifecycle
tasks.description: Triggers: pre-meeting (fire N minutes before an event), event-created, event-cancelled. Uses provider's push notifications where available (Google Calendar watch) otherwise polling.

tasks.title: Calendar provider abstraction (Google + Outlook)
tasks.description: Common Calendar provider interface. Google implementation via internal/integrations/google/. Outlook implementation via MS Graph OAuth. Per-connection selects provider at creation time.

tasks.title: Wire conversation_id through runtime + SSE + REST
tasks.description: Runtime attaches conversation_id to runs created via CreateRunFromSource. Domain events include conversation_id so SSE subscribers can filter. REST endpoints: list conversations, get conversation with runs, delete conversation.

tasks.title: Provider profile REST API
tasks.description: REST API endpoints: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /provider-profiles, GET/PUT /settings/llm-default. Include validation and proper error handling.

tasks.title: Code Reviewer assistant template + acceptance e2e
tasks.description: templates/code-reviewer.json: assistant template defaulting to a GitHub Connection, allowed tools = github.pulls.{list,get,comment,review} + llm.chat, system prompt focused on PR-review craft (what to flag, how to phrase, when to approve vs request-changes). UI Browse Templates gains the entry. End-to-end test (manual or e2e harness) walks the spec acceptance: user connects a PAT against a fixture repo, asks the Code Reviewer 'review the latest open PR on <repo>', the run reaches plan_review showing the planned tools, after approval the run produces a review comment draft pending user approval before posting via github.pulls.comment. Validates the full chain (auth → list → get → analyze → comment-as-write).

tasks.title: Add channel/connector selection to assistant UI
tasks.description: Update assistant form UI to show available connectors as channel options. Multi-select checkboxes or badges for selecting which connectors an assistant uses.

tasks.title: Audit runtime errors (ceiling, policy, approvals, retries, planner)
tasks.description: Rewrite runtime-level error messages: capability ceiling violations, policy denials, approval rejections, retry-budget exhaustion, plan-validation failures, tool not found, schema mismatch, step timeout, run cancellation.

tasks.title: DMG bundle + Homebrew Cask publish
tasks.description: Tauri produces a notarized .dmg via `tauri build --target universal-apple-darwin` after signing-05. New repo `nomiai/homebrew-tap` (or PR to homebrew-cask) with a `Casks/nomi.rb` cask file: url to the GitHub Release .dmg, sha256, version. Auto-bump cask on release via a small workflow that opens a PR to the tap repo with the new version + sha. Acceptance: `brew tap nomiai/tap && brew install --cask nomi` succeeds end-to-end on fresh macOS, app launches without Gatekeeper prompt.

tasks.title: plugin_state table + per-plugin enable/disable
tasks.description: New SQLite table tracking (plugin_id, distribution, installed, enabled, version, source_url, signature_fingerprint, installed_at). On first boot seed with one row per system plugin marked distribution=system, installed=true, enabled=true. pluginRegistry.StartAll consults enabled=true before invoking Plugin.Start(). New REST endpoints: PATCH /plugins/:id/state {enabled: bool}, GET /plugins/:id/state. Frontend: top-level enable toggle on each plugin card. System plugins surface the toggle as disabled with a 'system plugin, always available' tooltip. Smallest user-visible win and answers the original 'can I stop plugins?' question; ships independently before any WASM work.

tasks.title: Ed25519 update signing keypair + Tauri updater plugin config
tasks.description: Generate the Ed25519 keypair used to sign Tauri update manifests (`tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/nomi.key`). Document custody: private key stored in 1Password / equivalent; public key embedded in `app/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` under `plugins.updater.pubkey`. Install + configure `tauri-plugin-updater` v2 in `app/src-tauri/Cargo.toml` and the corresponding JS bridge in `app/src/`. Endpoint URL points at GitHub Releases (`https://github.com/felixgeelhaar/nomi/releases/latest/download/latest.json`). High priority because it gates the entire update flow.

tasks.title: Email ToolProvider: send, search, read
tasks.description: Tools: email.send (connection_id, to, subject, body, cc?, bcc?, attachments?), email.search (IMAP SEARCH with operators), email.read_message (full message with attachments). Capabilities registered in permission engine.

tasks.title: Discord rich-media outbound (message attachments)
tasks.description: Extend Discord's Channel.Send to use ChannelMessageSendComplex with MessageSend.Files populated from OutboundMessage.Attachments. In-message captions go into the Content field alongside.

tasks.title: UI: install confirmation dialog
tasks.description: When the user picks a marketplace plugin (or drag-drops a local .nomi-plugin), a modal surfaces: plugin name + publisher + version, README.md rendering, capability list (each capability is a chip with hover-card explaining what it grants), NetworkAllowlist (chip list of host patterns), signature trust status (green checkmark for hub-signed, red banner for unsigned dev bundles, yellow for signed-but-unknown-publisher). Install button calls POST /plugins/install. Cancel discards. Lives in app/src/components/plugin-install-dialog.tsx.

tasks.title: Runtime auto-enrichment pass for inbound attachments
tasks.description: Before planning starts, if the Run has attachments, the runtime consults per-kind enrichment config (e.g. audio → media.transcribe, image → media.describe_image) and invokes the configured tools to append their outputs into the Run's initial goal/input. Skips when the media plugin isn't installed. User-facing behavior: an inbound Telegram voice note becomes goal 'Alice: [voice transcript] ...' automatically.

tasks.title: Project scaffolding and directory structure
tasks.description: Initialize Go module (nomid), create directory structure (cmd/, internal/, pkg/, migrations/, web/), set up Tauri project in app/ directory. Create Makefile with common commands. Add .gitignore and basic README.

tasks.title: Capability-gated WASM host imports
tasks.description: Implement host_log, host_http_request (gated by network.outgoing + NetworkAllowlist intersection), host_filesystem_read/write (gated by filesystem.read/write + workspace_root), host_command_exec (gated by command.exec + allowed_binaries), host_secrets_get (gated by secrets.read), host_emit_event (always available). Imports the plugin's manifest doesn't declare are simply unavailable — the WASM module fails to load, surfaced as a clear install-time error. Per-call gate routes through permEngine.Evaluate so the user's PermissionPolicy still has the final say. Tests cover the deny path (manifest declared but policy denied), the allow path, and the missing-import path (plugin tries to call something the manifest didn't ask for).

tasks.title: Add file-tree preview component
tasks.description: Reusable component that calls the preview endpoint and displays a collapsible file tree for a given folder path. Used in assistant form and potentially run detail view.

tasks.title: GitHub TriggerProvider: webhook routing
tasks.description: Webhook receiver endpoint (validates X-Hub-Signature-256), routes events to trigger subscriptions: pr_opened, issue_commented, push, workflow_failed. Per-connection allowlist of event types.

tasks.title: Add connector REST API endpoints
tasks.description: Add REST endpoints for connector management: GET /connectors (list available), GET /connectors/:name/status, POST /connectors/:name/configure.

tasks.title: Gmail ToolProvider: send/search/read/label/archive
tasks.description: Tools: gmail.send (richer than SMTP: inline attachments, HTML, drafts), gmail.search_threads, gmail.read_thread, gmail.label, gmail.archive. Uses Gmail API rather than SMTP/IMAP for API-specific operations.

tasks.title: NetworkAllowlist on manifest + per-capability constraint enforcement
tasks.description: Add Requirements.NetworkAllowlist []string to PluginManifest. Bundled plugins gain explicit lists (Telegram: api.telegram.org + *.t.me; Slack: slack.com + *.slack.com + *.slack-edge.com; Discord: discord.com + *.discord.com + *.discordapp.net; Calendar: www.googleapis.com + oauth2.googleapis.com; Email: empty, runtime supplements with per-Connection imap_host/smtp_host). Wildcard matching mirrors permissions.matchWildcard (leading-dot anchor; *.slack.com does NOT match slack.com.attacker.com). For bundled plugins this is informational + surfaces in the install/info dialog; for marketplace WASM plugins it gates host_http_request at the WASM boundary in lifecycle-04.

tasks.title: Promote Calendar to marketplace as dogfooding test
tasks.description: Repackage the Calendar plugin (currently bundled as system) as a WASM .nomi-plugin bundle published to NomiHub. Validates the entire pipeline end-to-end: WASM compilation of a real plugin with HTTP + OAuth needs, signing on the GitHub Actions side, install via UI, capability + NetworkAllowlist intersection at runtime, version-bump via update flow. Calendar stays bundled for users who don't want NomiHub-fetched plugins; the marketplace version is the canonical update target going forward. Acceptance: user with a fresh install can search 'calendar' in the marketplace browser, install it, configure a Google account, list events — all without rebuilding the daemon.

tasks.title: Error Message Audit — Plain-Language, Actionable, Non-Technical
tasks.description: Cross-cutting V1.3 audit: rewrite every user-visible error message to explain (a) what went wrong in non-technical language, (b) why it happened, and (c) what the user can do next. Add structured code/title/body/action envelope where missing.

tasks.title: Port Google OAuth to internal/integrations/google/
tasks.description: Move google.OAuthManager + refresh-token handling out of internal/connectors/google/ into internal/integrations/google/. Shared by Gmail and Calendar plugins. Device-flow endpoints move to a plugin-agnostic Google-connection flow reused by both plugins.

tasks.title: Email ChannelProvider + threading
tasks.description: Email plugin registers as ChannelProvider per enabled connection. Inbound mail → Conversation (keyed by Message-ID / References chain) → Run. Outbound replies use In-Reply-To + References to keep threading. Identity allowlist integration.

tasks.title: Reliability Eval Suite and Failure Tests
tasks.description: Build planner/tool reliability eval harness with failure taxonomy, cross-plugin contract tests, and end-to-end failure-mode scenarios with regression gates.

tasks.title: Provider profile settings UI
tasks.description: Build settings page UI for managing provider profiles: list, create, edit, delete provider profiles with form fields for name, type, endpoint, models, credentials.

tasks.title: github.issues tools
tasks.description: Implement four issue-shaped tools on the GitHub plugin: github.issues.list (filterable by repo / state / assignee / labels, paginated), github.issues.get (single issue with body + comments), github.issues.create (new issue; permission-gated since it's a write), github.issues.comment (append comment; permission-gated). Each tool ResolvesPermission against the connection's PAT/App scope at call time so a token without `issues: write` cleanly errors instead of producing a 403 from upstream. Table-driven tests cover 200/403/404/422 GitHub API responses + rate-limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining=0 surfaces as a typed RateLimitError so the runtime can back off rather than fail the run).

tasks.title: Cert custody + rotation runbook
tasks.description: New `docs/runbooks/signing-certs.md` documenting: (1) Apple Developer ID Application cert renewal cadence (yearly), revocation procedure, where the .p12 + password live (1Password vault path), how to roll the GitHub Actions secrets without breaking in-flight releases. (2) Windows EV/OV cert renewal + rotation. (3) Ed25519 update signing key custody — who has the private key, what happens if it's lost (forced re-install for all users, plan a rotation strategy now). Includes a calendar reminder template and an incident playbook for 'cert leaked' / 'cert expired during release'. Cheap to write, expensive to skip.

tasks.title: Slack app integration (Socket Mode)
tasks.description: Slack app auth: bot token + app-level token. Events API delivered via Socket Mode WebSocket (no inbound webhook hosting needed). Per-workspace connection model.

tasks.title: nomid --version + Tauri build-time version injection
tasks.description: Add `nomid --version` flag to cmd/nomid/main.go that prints semver + commit hash + build date. Wire commit/version through ldflags in Makefile (`-ldflags="-X main.version=$(VERSION) -X main.commit=$(COMMIT) -X main.buildDate=$(DATE)"`). On the Tauri side, expose the same via a `app_version` Tauri command consumed by an About panel in Settings. Required by the auto-update flow (signing-03) and the release workflow (signing-04) so users and CI can both ask 'what version is this binary?'. No external dependencies; lowest-cost starting task.

tasks.title: Discord ToolProvider: post/react/manage_roles
tasks.description: Tools: discord.post_message (channel/user), discord.react (emoji reactions), discord.manage_roles (add/remove roles, subject to bot scope + guild permissions).

tasks.title: Ed25519 signing + verification chain
tasks.description: Embed NomiHub root public key (Ed25519) in the daemon binary at build time via go:embed. Bundle reader verifies signature.ed25519 over the canonical SHA-256 of (manifest.json || plugin.wasm). Publisher keys are signed by the root key — verifier walks the chain publisher → root before trusting the bundle. Defends against compromised hub.nomi.ai serving altered bytes via content-addressed hashes recorded in the catalog. Reject expired publisher keys. Cosign/Sigstore deferred per ADR open question; revisit when plugin volume grows.

tasks.title: Assistant advanced mode model override UI
tasks.description: Update assistant form with advanced mode toggle. When advanced mode is enabled, show ModelPolicy configuration: preferred provider/model selection, fallback options, local-only toggle.

tasks.title: plugins.Registry + role-view projections
tasks.description: plugins.Registry as source of truth. Role-view registries (channels, tools, triggers) are projections built via type-assertion at Register time. Register a plugin that plays multiple roles and verify its contributions fan out.

tasks.title: Run and Step state machines
tasks.description: Implement state machine logic for Run and Step lifecycles. Define valid transitions and guard conditions. Create StateMachine interface and implementations. Include transition validation and error handling.

tasks.title: Add provider_profiles table migration
tasks.description: Create provider_profiles table migration with id, name, type, endpoint, model_ids JSON, secret_ref, enabled, created_at columns.

tasks.title: Define Connector interface and Manifest types
tasks.description: Define Connector interface with Start(), Stop(), SendMessage(), Name(), Manifest() methods. Define ConnectorManifest with name, version, capabilities, config schema.

tasks.title: Windows MSI build + EV/OV codesign + Scoop/winget
tasks.description: Tauri WiX MSI target on the windows-latest runner. Codesign with EV or OV cert (DigiCert / Sectigo) imported via WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE_P12 + password secrets, using `signtool sign /tr http://timestamp.digicert.com /td sha256 /fd sha256`. Publish manifests: Scoop bucket (separate repo `nomiai/scoop-bucket` with auto-bumped manifest), winget submission (PR to microsoft/winget-pkgs with the new version). Blocked-until: Windows code-signing cert procurement ($200–$700/yr depending on EV vs OV + vendor). Acceptance: `scoop install nomi` works on a clean Windows 11 VM with no SmartScreen warning.

tasks.title: Canonical Error Envelope Everywhere
tasks.description: Standardize all API/UI error responses to a canonical shape (code/title/message/action/details), remove ad-hoc payloads, and enforce deterministic rendering across core flows.

tasks.title: ProviderProfile domain model and repository
tasks.description: Create ProviderProfile domain model, repository with CRUD operations, and global settings storage for default provider.

tasks.title: Identity REST + audit events
tasks.description: REST endpoints: list/create/update/delete identities per connection. Event emissions for identity lifecycle and first-contact events (allowlist_matched, first_contact_dropped, first_contact_approval_queued) so the UI can show activity.

tasks.title: Plugins tab (replaces Connections)
tasks.description: New Plugins tab in Settings. Per-plugin card shows manifest (name, version, roles), connection list, 'Add connection' button. Per-plugin auth flows: OAuth device flow (Google), OAuth web redirect (Slack/Discord/GitHub), bot token entry (Telegram), IMAP credentials (Email generic).

tasks.title: GitHub Actions tag-driven release workflow
tasks.description: New `.github/workflows/release.yml` triggered on `v*.*.*` tags. Matrix: macos-14 (universal binary via lipo of arm64 + x86_64), ubuntu-latest, windows-latest. Per-job: install Go + Node + pnpm/npm, run `make build` (Go daemon) + `make app-build` (Tauri bundle). Outputs uploaded as workflow artifacts in this task — signing + publishing happens in 05/07/08. Creates the GitHub Release as a draft on tag push; subsequent platform jobs attach binaries. Concurrency group ensures only one release run per tag. High priority — every other distribution task depends on it.

tasks.title: Slack ToolProvider: post/search/react
tasks.description: Tools: slack.post_message (to channel or user, with threading), slack.search_messages (across workspace subject to app scopes), slack.react_to_message (add/remove reactions).

tasks.title: github.repos.file_read + clone tools
tasks.description: Two read-shaped tools: (1) github.repos.file_read fetches a single file via the contents API at a given ref (default: main); permission-gated as 'read remote contents' since the auth scope already implies the user trusted us with read access. Streams + size-caps at 1MiB to keep agents from accidentally swallowing a 100MiB CSV. (2) github.repos.clone shells out to `git clone --depth 1` into the assistant's workspace (filesystem.write capability + workspace_root sandboxing — same path-resolution helper used by Obsidian/folder-context). file_read covers ~80% of code-reading flows without paying the clone overhead; clone is the escape hatch for cross-file reasoning.

tasks.title: Obsidian ToolProvider: create/update/search/link
tasks.description: Tools: obsidian.create_note (respects frontmatter templates), obsidian.update_note, obsidian.search_notes (text + tag + link-graph search), obsidian.link_notes (create [[wikilink]] between two notes).

tasks.title: End-to-end test of folder context flow
tasks.description: Verify that creating an assistant with folder context, then starting a run, correctly loads the folder tree into the run. Check events and step output.

tasks.title: Security Consistency and Threat Model Refresh
tasks.description: Perform focused threat-model refresh and control review for plugin install/update/uninstall, identity allowlists, secrets handling, and runtime tool boundaries; produce prioritized remediations.

tasks.title: .nomi-plugin bundle format
tasks.description: Define the gzipped-tar bundle layout: manifest.json + plugin.wasm + README.md + signature.ed25519 + publisher.json. Bundle reader package internal/plugins/bundle/ that opens, validates structural integrity (required files present, manifest parseable, content-addressed bundle hash matches), and returns the parsed manifest + WASM bytes for downstream loaders. publisher.json carries publisher name + their public key fingerprint chained to the NomiHub root key. Test fixtures: a hand-built valid bundle, a bundle with a corrupt manifest, a bundle missing the signature.

tasks.title: Update detection + explicit update action
tasks.description: Daemon polls NomiHub catalog daily (configurable via app_settings). plugin_state gains available_version + last_checked_at columns; populated when catalog has a higher semver than installed. New event types plugin.update_available + plugin.updated. POST /plugins/:id/update fetches the latest bundle, verifies signature, atomically swaps the installed module (drains any in-flight tool calls first), and bumps plugin_state.version. Explicit only — no silent updates. Tests cover: catalog has newer version → flagged; user triggers update → swap succeeds; signature failure → swap aborted with installed version preserved.

tasks.title: Slack rich-media outbound (files.uploadV2)
tasks.description: Extend Slack's Channel.Send to upload attachments via files.uploadV2 into the target channel (+ thread_ts for threading). Falls back to slack.MsgOptionText when there are no attachments.

tasks.title: Implement ConnectorRegistry
tasks.description: Create ConnectorRegistry that manages connector lifecycle: Register(), StartAll(), StopAll(), RouteMessage(). Store connector instances and handle graceful startup/shutdown.

tasks.title: UI: NomiHub marketplace browser
tasks.description: New 'Browse plugins' button in the Plugins tab opens a dedicated panel that lists the NomiHub catalog. Search by name / capability / publisher. Each catalog entry is a card with name + description + publisher + capability badges + 'Install' button. Already-installed plugins show 'Installed' instead of 'Install' with a link back to their card. Consumes GET /plugins/marketplace. Lives in app/src/components/plugin-marketplace.tsx.

tasks.title: com.nomi.media plugin scaffold + manifest
tasks.description: New tool-only plugin at internal/plugins/media/. Manifest declares tools: media.speak (TTS), media.transcribe (STT), media.describe_image (vision), with capability strings media.tts / media.stt / media.vision. Cardinality single — one install, no per-connection account concept. ConfigSchema fields for backend selection (tts_backend, stt_model_path, etc.) so users configure all media once in one card.

tasks.title: Auto-update end-to-end smoke test
tasks.description: Script that builds version vN, installs it on a fresh runner (macOS + Windows + Linux matrix where possible), publishes vN+1 to a fixture release, and asserts that the running app detects the update, downloads it, and the relaunched binary reports the new version via `nomid --version`. Lives in `.github/workflows/update-e2e.yml` triggered manually + on release. Catches regressions in the updater plugin config, manifest format, or signature verification path. Acceptance: green CI run that exercises the full update path on at least macOS.

tasks.title: Calendar ToolProvider: list/create/update/delete/find_free
tasks.description: Tools: calendar.list_upcoming (range-filtered), calendar.create_event, calendar.update_event, calendar.delete_event, calendar.find_free_slot (duration + participants → candidate slots).

tasks.title: latest.json manifest format + silent download + relaunch banner
tasks.description: Define the Tauri updater `latest.json` manifest schema (version, notes, pub_date, platforms.{darwin-aarch64,darwin-x86_64,linux-x86_64,windows-x86_64}.{signature, url}). Implement the React update UX: useUpdater hook checks on app boot + every 6h, downloads silently in the background, shows a non-modal banner 'New version ready. Relaunch now?' with Relaunch / Later buttons. Defer is sticky for 24h. Lives in `app/src/components/update-banner.tsx` + `app/src/lib/updater.ts`. Acceptance: a manually-edited fake `latest.json` triggers the banner end-to-end against a local file:// URL.

tasks.title: Repository pattern for SQLite operations
tasks.description: Implement repository layer for each domain: RunRepository, StepRepository, AssistantRepository, EventRepository, MemoryRepository. Include CRUD operations and transaction support. Write tests for repositories.

tasks.title: GitHub Connections UI
tasks.description: Connections tab gains a 'Add GitHub' card. Two paths: (a) PAT — opens the GitHub fine-grained PAT generation page in the system browser, user pastes the token, frontend POSTs to /plugins/github/connections with token + selected repo allowlist; backend stores via secrets.Store and round-trips the result. (b) GitHub App install — uses github-01's installation flow (open https://github.com/apps/<appname>/installations/new in browser, capture redirect with installation_id). Per-connection: org/login chip, repo allowlist editor, polling enable toggle, last-poll-success indicator. Lives in app/src/components/connections/github.tsx + a useGithubConnections query hook.

tasks.title: NomiHub catalog format + scaffold repo
tasks.description: Define https://hub.nomi.ai/index.json schema (signed catalog: array of {plugin_id, name, latest_version, publisher, manifest_summary, capabilities, network_allowlist, install_size_bytes, sha256, signature, readme_excerpt}). Scaffold the nomiai/hub repo with: a GitHub Actions workflow that builds + signs new bundles on PR merge, a curated example plugin (Calendar promoted from bundled — same code base, repackaged as a marketplace bundle), and the GitHub Pages config to serve index.json + bundle blobs. Daemon-side: scheduled fetcher in internal/plugins/hub/ pulls index.json daily, caches in app-data, exposes via GET /plugins/marketplace.

tasks.title: Install / uninstall REST endpoints
tasks.description: POST /plugins/install accepting either a URL (fetch from NomiHub or arbitrary HTTPS) or a multipart-uploaded local .nomi-plugin file. Body validates via lifecycle-05 + lifecycle-06; on success the bundle is unpacked into ~/.nomi/plugins/<id>/, plugin_state row inserted with distribution=marketplace + installed=true + enabled=false, plugin module loaded into the registry. DELETE /plugins/:id with optional cascade=true query param: cascade=false (default) keeps plugin_connections + bindings + identities so reinstall reattaches; cascade=true removes everything including credentials in secrets.Store. System plugins refuse uninstall with 409. Tests cover happy path (install → enable → disable → uninstall), cascade vs non-cascade uninstall, signature failure, and version-conflict-on-reinstall.

tasks.title: Provider presets + Plugins-tab UX
tasks.description: UI presets for Gmail OAuth, Outlook OAuth, Fastmail app-password, iCloud, Yahoo, and generic IMAP/SMTP. Preset wizard derives IMAP/SMTP hosts from the email address when possible. OAuth flows via internal/integrations/ where applicable.

tasks.title: Email TriggerProvider: filtered inbox watch
tasks.description: Inbox-watch triggers fire runs on inbound messages matching user-defined filters (from, subject contains, label). Per-connection trigger rules persisted. Rate-limited so a mail storm can't flood the run queue.

tasks.title: Whisper STT backend for media.transcribe
tasks.description: Implement media.transcribe using whisper.cpp. Input: audio bytes + language hint. Output: transcript text. Models live under ~/.nomi/models/whisper/; plugin handles download with progress events. Sandboxed subprocess. Auto-invoked by the inbound-enrichment pass on voice attachments.

tasks.title: Slack ChannelProvider + Block Kit approvals
tasks.description: DM to bot → Conversation + Run. Threaded replies via thread_ts. Approval rendering as Block Kit interactive buttons; approval resolution via button click posts back through the same socket. Identity allowlist integration.

tasks.title: Polling-based issue/PR event triggers
tasks.description: GitHub plugin background loop polls each enabled Connection's repo allowlist every 60s for new issues + PR review-requests. Emits trigger.fired events that the runtime ingests via the existing trigger framework (same path Calendar/Slack use). Polling state (last_etag, last_seen_id per repo) persists in plugin-private rows so a daemon restart doesn't replay 30-day-old issues as new. Webhook-receiver path is the V2 upgrade — flagged in the manifest under `triggers[].mode = 'polling'` for the UI to surface; switching to webhooks happens once we have a tunneled receiver story. Conditional-request If-None-Match cuts API quota by ~80% in the steady state.

tasks.title: Tool interface and registry
tasks.description: Define Tool interface (Name, Execute). Create ToolRegistry for tool discovery and lookup. Implement tool registration and capability mapping. Add tool execution wrapper with logging and error handling.

tasks.title: Integration test for connector registry
tasks.description: Integration test that verifies connector registry starts/stops connectors correctly and routes messages.

tasks.title: macOS codesign + notarization + universal binary
tasks.description: Apple Developer ID Application cert imported into the macos-14 runner via encrypted GitHub secret (APPLE_CERTIFICATE_P12 + APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD). Build universal binary with `lipo -create` of arm64 + x86_64 builds. `codesign --deep --options=runtime --entitlements app/src-tauri/entitlements.plist` over the .app bundle. Submit to Apple notarytool with APPLE_ID + APPLE_TEAM_ID + APPLE_APP_PASSWORD secrets; staple ticket on success. Hard gate: Gatekeeper-approved on a fresh macOS install with no warnings. Blocked-until: Apple Developer Program enrollment ($99/yr) is in place — flag the task description if the cert isn't yet provisioned.

tasks.title: Browser ToolProvider: navigate/observe/interact/extract
tasks.description: Tools: browser.navigate, browser.observe (returns labeled DOM snapshot), browser.click, browser.type, browser.screenshot, browser.extract (CSS-selector-based text/attribute extraction). Stateless per call; optional session id for multi-step flows.

tasks.title: GitHub ToolProvider: PRs/issues/code
tasks.description: Tools: github.open_pr, github.comment_pr, github.comment_issue, github.create_issue, github.search_code, github.read_file. All scoped to the connection's installed repos.

tasks.title: Vault discovery + FS sandboxing
tasks.description: Per-Connection vault path. Filesystem sandboxing prevents writes outside the declared vault root (uses existing ResolveWithinRoot helper). Vault metadata: detected .obsidian config, note count, tag list.

tasks.title: Email rich-media outbound (MIME multipart/mixed)
tasks.description: Extend Email's Channel.Send to construct a multipart/mixed MIME body when attachments are present: text/plain body + base64-encoded attachment parts with Content-Disposition + Content-Type headers. Transport stays net/smtp for port 587 STARTTLS; port 465 TLS dialer already handles the framing.

tasks.title: Add ModelPolicy to assistant definition
tasks.description: Add ModelPolicy to AssistantDefinition with mode, preferred model, fallback, local_only, allow_fallback fields. Update assistant API to accept model_policy.

tasks.title: github.pulls tools
tasks.description: Implement github.pulls.list / get / comment / review. .review submits a PR review (approve / request changes / comment); .comment leaves a single inline or top-level comment. Review-as-an-action is permission-gated, comment is permission-gated separately so users can grant comment-but-not-approve. .get returns the unified diff + linked issue refs + checkrun status so a Code Reviewer assistant has everything it needs in one round-trip without the agent re-asking for the diff. Tests assert pagination behavior on .list (>30 PRs returns full set), and that .review with `body: ''` doesn't accidentally submit an empty 'approve'.

tasks.title: Telegram rich-media outbound
tasks.description: Extend Telegram's Channel.Send to dispatch attachments through the right Bot API method per Attachment.Kind: sendPhoto (image), sendDocument (doc/other), sendVoice (audio with is_voice=true), sendVideo (video). Caption handled via the caption field. Preserve threading via reply_to_message_id when an external conversation id maps to a prior message.

tasks.title: Core domain model definitions
tasks.description: Define Go structs for Run, Step, AssistantDefinition, ContextAttachment, PermissionPolicy, PermissionRule, MemoryEntry, Event. Include JSON marshaling and validation. Create enum types for RunStatus, StepStatus, EventType.

tasks.title: Piper TTS backend for media.speak
tasks.description: Implement media.speak using Piper ONNX runtime. Input: text + optional voice model id. Output: audio bytes (wav or ogg). Model files live under ~/.nomi/models/piper/*.onnx; plugin handles download on first use with a progress event. Runs as a subprocess via internal/tools/sandbox so the plugin never shells out unsandboxed.

tasks.title: Audit UI error surfaces (API errors, network, toasts)
tasks.description: Rewrite frontend error messages: network errors reaching daemon, vite preview vs Tauri bridge mismatch, plugin install corrupted bundle, signature verification failed, generic API error fallbacks.

tasks.title: Assistant templates (2-3 built-in)
tasks.description: Create 2-3 built-in assistant templates: General Assistant (file operations, commands), Code Reviewer (git context, code analysis), DevOps Helper (command execution, file editing). Store as JSON files.

tasks.title: Frontend API types and client for providers
tasks.description: Add provider API client functions to frontend api.ts. Add ProviderProfile and ModelPolicy types to api.ts.

tasks.title: Headless-browser driver choice + sandbox
tasks.description: Evaluate chromedp vs playwright-go vs reusing Tauri WebView for automation. Sandbox design: separate process, isolated profile per assistant, no access to user's real browser cookies. Spike + decision doc before implementation.

tasks.title: Integration testing and end-to-end validation
tasks.description: Write integration tests for the full flow: create run → execute steps → approval → completion. Test tool execution, permission evaluation, and event emission. Validate API endpoints.

tasks.title: GitHub plugin scaffold + manifest
tasks.description: Scaffold internal/plugins/github/ as a system-tier plugin: manifest declares network.outgoing (api.github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com), filesystem.write (workspace-scoped only, for clone tools), and per-Connection auth slot. Plugin registers in cmd/nomid/main.go alongside other tier-1 plugins (Telegram, Slack, Discord, Calendar, Gmail). Connection table mirrors the existing pattern: plugin_connections row holds either an encrypted PAT or a GitHub App installation_id (signed off in github-01). DB migration adds github_connections sub-table for App-flavor metadata (org login, repo allowlist) since plugin_connections.metadata is JSON-blob-y. Empty tools list at this stage; subsequent tasks fill it in.

tasks.title: Gmail TriggerProvider: label/from/query watch
tasks.description: Triggers: label-watch (fire when a message gets a label), from-watch (fire on messages from specific senders), query-watch (fire on arbitrary Gmail-search-syntax matches). Uses Gmail history API for efficient delta polling.

tasks.title: Identity allowlist UI + first-contact review
tasks.description: Per-connection identities list in the Plugins tab with add/remove. First-contact policy toggle per connection (drop silently | reply 'request access' | queue approval). Approval queue view for pending first-contact requests.

tasks.title: UI: top-level plugin enable toggle
tasks.description: Plugins-tab card gains a top-level enable/disable toggle (separate from per-connection toggles). Disabled plugins show greyed cards with the description amended: 'no inbound polling, no outbound capability'. System plugins render the toggle as locked-on with a 'system plugin, always available' tooltip. Lives in app/src/components/plugins-manager.tsx; consumes PATCH /plugins/:id/state. Mutation invalidates the plugins query so other clients see the change immediately via SSE-driven cache.

tasks.title: Connection + AssistantConnectionBinding entities
tasks.description: New domain entities Connection and AssistantConnectionBinding. SQLite migrations (up + down). Repositories with CRUD + queries (list connections by plugin, list bindings by assistant, lookup connection by id). Secrets live in secrets.Store; plugin_connections holds only CredentialRefs.

tasks.title: GitHub App installation auth
tasks.description: GitHub App (not PAT) auth. Per-Connection installation id + installation access token minted on-demand. Repo scope configured per connection; bot avatar and user-visible attribution on comments/PRs.

tasks.title: IMAP receive loop + SMTP send client
tasks.description: Generic IMAP receive loop with IDLE support (fall back to polling when IDLE unsupported). SMTP send with STARTTLS + implicit TLS. Per-connection credentials via secrets.Store. No plugin wiring yet — just the transport layer in internal/plugins/email/transport/.

tasks.title: Folder context attachment implementation
tasks.description: Implement folder context scanning and indexing. Add context attachment to runs. Build file tree representation for UI. Integrate with filesystem tools.

tasks.title: Conversation entity + migration
tasks.description: New Conversation domain entity with (id, plugin_id, connection_id, channel_conversation_id, identity_id, assistant_id, created/updated_at). SQLite migration adds plugin_conversations table and conversation_id column on runs (nullable).

tasks.title: Event system implementation
tasks.description: Create event bus with SQLite persistence. Implement EventPublisher and EventSubscriber interfaces. Support filtering by event type and run ID. Include event struct validation.

tasks.title: Assistant edit view: build-my-agent composer
tasks.description: Assistant edit view gains grouped-by-plugin checklist of Connections, filterable by role. Default 1-per-slot with 'Add another' that reveals primary marker + per-connection policy override dropdown. Edits persist to assistant_connection_bindings; save triggers re-intersection of manifest permissions.

tasks.title: Integration tests for provider configuration
tasks.description: Integration test verifying provider profile CRUD, global default setting, and assistant model policy storage.

tasks.title: Roady State Governance Normalization
tasks.description: Fix Roady task-ID drift and special-character transition issues; add transition integrity checks and stale in_progress cleanup automation.

tasks.title: Implement real Telegram connector
tasks.description: Replace the mock Telegram connector with a real implementation using go-telegram-bot-api or tgbot library. Implement polling loop, message handling, and SendMessage with real HTTP calls to Telegram Bot API.

tasks.title: Per-connection health status + event-driven badges
tasks.description: Plugins-tab cards show per-connection status: auth healthy, last-event timestamp, error-since-last-event count. Badges driven by SSE events (connection_auth_failed, connection_event_received, connection_error) rather than polling.

tasks.title: Assistant/Policy UX Coherence Pass
tasks.description: Run UX coherence pass across assistant builder, policy, and approval surfaces with progressive disclosure and plain-language, actionable guidance.

tasks.title: Chats tab: one tab per Conversation
tasks.description: Chats tab lists Conversations grouped by plugin+connection. Selecting one shows the run history within that thread. Desktop-initiated runs without a conversation stay in a 'Local' group for backward compatibility.

tasks.title: Migrate Telegram to plugins/telegram/
tasks.description: Port internal/connectors/telegram.go to internal/plugins/telegram/ implementing Plugin + ChannelProvider. One-shot SQLite migration: existing connector_configs.config Connections → plugin_connections rows (preserving ids + default_assistant_id). Existing DefaultAssistantID becomes a role=channel binding. No behavior change for existing Telegram users.

tasks.title: Builder inline validation for ceiling violations
tasks.description: Add client-side validation in assistant-manager.tsx: when a policy rule references a capability family not in the ceiling, show inline error 'Tick the {Family} box above to allow this rule to take effect' and highlight the relevant capability checkbox.

tasks.title: Linux AppImage + .deb + SHA256SUMS
tasks.description: Tauri builds AppImage + .deb on ubuntu-latest. Generate SHA256SUMS file containing checksums for every artifact in the release (cross-platform). No code signing — Linux distros rely on package-manager trust + checksums. Publish .deb via a simple GitHub-Pages-hosted apt repo at apt.nomi.ai (Phase 4) or skip apt repo for v1 and rely on direct .deb download from GitHub Releases. AppImage is the primary path — single-file, executable, no install required. SHA256SUMS attached to the release.

tasks.title: Inbound attachment capture on channels
tasks.description: Extend each channel plugin's inbound handler to capture attachment metadata (kind, filename, content-type, url/bytes) on received messages. Plumb into the Run's initial input via a new RunInput.Attachments field (or via a sidecar table keyed by run_id if the runtime avoids growing Run row shape). Channel plugins don't fetch the bytes eagerly; they pass references that the inbound-enrichment pass resolves on demand.

tasks.title: Dev plugin loader (~/.nomi/plugins-dev/)
tasks.description: Settings flag dev_plugins_enabled (default false in production builds). When enabled, daemon loads unsigned .nomi-plugin bundles from ~/.nomi/plugins-dev/ at boot. UI surfaces dev-loaded plugins with a red 'unsigned, development build' banner. Allows plugin authors to iterate locally without going through NomiHub. Must NOT auto-load when the flag flips back to false (require restart). Test: drop a fixture bundle in the dev dir, flip the flag, verify it loads with the warning badge.

tasks.title: ChannelIdentity entity + enforcement
tasks.description: New ChannelIdentity entity with (id, plugin_id, connection_id, external_identifier, display_name, allowed_assistants, enabled). SQLite migration. Enforcement hook at channel-plugin inbound: unknown sender → check first-contact policy for this connection.

tasks.title: Run execution engine
tasks.description: Build the core runtime that orchestrates runs. Implement planning phase, step creation, step execution loop, and completion handling. Integrate with state machine, permission system, and tool registry. Handle errors and retries.

tasks.title: UI: update notifications + per-plugin update button
tasks.description: When plugin_state.available_version > version, the plugin card shows an 'Update available: vX.Y.Z' banner with a button. Clicking surfaces a diff modal (changes since installed version pulled from the catalog's release notes) and an 'Update now' confirm. Calls POST /plugins/:id/update. Globally, the Plugins tab nav-item shows a small dot when ANY plugin has an update pending. Mirrors the SSE-driven invalidation pattern so multiple windows stay in sync.

tasks.title: Add folder picker to assistant form UI
tasks.description: Add a folder path input (with directory picker button using Tauri dialog API) to the assistant creation/management form. Allow attaching multiple folders, showing path + name.

tasks.title: Core tool implementations
tasks.description: Implement filesystem.read, filesystem.write, and command.exec tools. Add proper error handling, input validation, and capability tags. Test tools with temporary directories. Ensure sandboxing for command execution.

tasks.title: Plugin + role interfaces
tasks.description: Define Plugin interface, role interfaces (ChannelProvider, ToolProvider, TriggerProvider, ContextSourceProvider), PluginManifest, Contributions types in new internal/plugins/ package. No registry yet — just the types.
